t1: We tested mini-STRC (616-1775) against every relevant cochlear partner. No direct protein-protein interaction was detected. Critically, full-length STRC also doesn't interact in these assays, confirming that negative results are not truncation artifacts but a known limitation of AF3's complex prediction mechanism. Li et al. (2026) showed that AF3 assembles complexes through interface-level geometric pattern matching learned from training data, not coevolution (bioRxiv). For non-canonical interfaces (membrane-associated, glycan-mediated), AF3 lacks structural priors, producing low ipTM scores even for real interactions. The shorter construct (700-1775) is a strict subset of mini-STRC: it removes 84 more N-terminal residues that are disordered in all models. Interaction results transfer directly.
t2: Key insight: The positive control (NFAT + Calcineurin, ipTM 0.73-0.91) proves our methodology works — and illustrates exactly the mechanism described by Li et al. (2026): AF3 excels at canonical interfaces with strong geometric priors in training data. The negative results across all STRC partners reflect a distributional mismatch: stereocilin's membrane-associated and glycan-mediated interactions are structurally atypical, analogous to the antigen-antibody limitation Li et al. identified. Low ipTM here is not evidence of non-interaction — it is evidence of AF3's interface prior gap.
t18: Also no dimer — truncation didn't cause this
